protocol.h(3) Library Functions Manual protocol.h(3)

NAME

protocol.h - ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int protocol_start_connection ()
Start protocol connection handling. void protocol_stop_connection ()
Stop protocol connection handling. bool protocol_connection_lost ()
Check if connection has been lost. const acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()
Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

Defines the protocol handling interface for client-side packet processing and data reception thread management.

The protocol handler manages the data reception thread, which continuously receives packets from the server, decrypts them, and dispatches them to appropriate handlers based on packet type. It also detects connection loss and signals the main thread to initiate reconnection.

Command Types Handled

The client processes these command/packet types from the server:

  • PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME: Terminal display frame (rendered to stdout)
  • PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH: Opus-encoded audio samples (sent to audio playback)
  • PACKET_TYPE_PONG: Keepalive response (recorded for timeout detection)
  • PACKET_TYPE_CLEAR_CONSOLE: Terminal clear command (resets display)
  • PACKET_TYPE_SERVER_STATE: Server state updates (logged for debugging)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

See also

topic_client_protocol "Protocol Architecture"

Definition in file src/client/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

bool protocol_connection_lost ()

Check if connection has been lost. Atomically checks whether the data reception thread has detected connection loss. Connection loss is detected by:

  • Socket read error (recv() returns error)
  • Socket closed by server (recv() returns 0)
  • Decryption failure (corrupted stream)
  • Invalid packet magic number (out of sync)

Once loss is detected, this flag remains true until the next successful connection.

Returns

true if connection lost (detected by protocol thread), false otherwise

Note

This is checked by the main thread to trigger reconnection logic. The flag is set atomically by the protocol thread and checked atomically by main.

See also

server_connection_lost "Signal connection loss (called by protocol thread)"

Check if connection has been lost

Returns

true if protocol detected connection loss, false otherwise

Definition at line 1385 of file client/protocol.c.

1385                                 {
1386   return atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited) || server_connection_is_lost();
1387 }




References atomic_load_bool(), and
    server_connection_is_lost().






const
  acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()


Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch. Returns the packet
    dispatch callbacks structure used to route received packets to appropriate
    handlers. This is primarily used for ACDS signaling packet dispatch and
    WebRTC peer negotiation.




The callbacks structure includes handlers for:


    
  
  • ASCII frame rendering
    • 
  
  • Audio batch processing
    • 
  
  • Server state updates
    • 
  
  • Connection control commands (clear, reset, etc.)
    • 



Returns


Pointer to client callbacks structure (never NULL)


Used by WebRTC sessions to receive and dispatch ACDS signaling
    packets.




Returns pointer to the global callback structure for use by WebRTC
    sessions that need to receive ACDS signaling packets.




Returns


Pointer to client callbacks (never NULL)


Definition at line 980 of file
  client/protocol.c.


980                                                              {
981   return &g_acip_client_callbacks;
982 }










int
  protocol_start_connection ()


Start protocol connection handling. Spawns the data reception
    thread in the global client worker thread pool. The thread begins receiving
    and processing packets from the server. Must be called after the server
    connection is established and the cryptographic handshake is complete.




The thread will:



  
1.

  
Continuously receive packets from the server socket

  
2.

  
Decrypt packets using the negotiated session key

  
3.

  
Dispatch packets to appropriate handlers based on type

  
4.

  
Detect connection loss and signal the main thread




Returns


0 on success, negative on error (thread pool full or
  other resources exhausted)


Note


Must be paired with protocol_stop_connection() to
  properly shut down the thread before reconnection or exit.


See also


protocol_stop_connection "Stop data reception
  thread"



protocol_connection_lost "Check if connection was
    lost"




Start protocol connection handling




Initializes protocol state and starts the data reception thread.
    Must be called after successful server connection establishment.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1178 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1178                                 {
1179   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🟢 PROTOCOL_START: Starting client protocol initialization");
1180 
1181   // Register protocol atomics with named debug registry
1182   static bool protocol_atomics_registered = false;
1183   if (!protocol_atomics_registered) {
1184     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_data_thread_exited, "protocol_data_reception_thread_exit_confirmation", NULL);
1185     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_frames_rendered, "protocol_frames_successfully_rendered", NULL);
1186     protocol_atomics_registered = true;
1187   }
1188 
1189   // Reset protocol state for new connection
1190   g_server_state_initialized = false;
1191   g_last_active_count = 0;
1192   g_should_clear_before_next_frame = false;
1193 
1194   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STATE_RESET: server_initialized=false, active_count=0, clear_flag=false");
1195 
1196   // Reset display state for new connection
1197   display_reset_for_new_connection();
1198 
1199   // Send CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet FIRST before starting any threads
1200   // Server expects this as the first packet after crypto handshake
1201   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 📤 SENDING_CAPABILITIES: terminal_size negotiation");
1202   asciichat_error_t cap_result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
1203                                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
1204   if (cap_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1205     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ CAPABILITIES_FAILED: cannot send terminal size");
1206     return -1;
1207   }
1208   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ CAPABILITIES_SENT: terminal_size sent successfully");
1209 
1210   // Send STREAM_START packet with combined stream types BEFORE starting worker threads
1211   // This tells the server what streams to expect before any data arrives
1212   uint32_t stream_types = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO; // Always have video
1213   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1214     stream_types |= STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO; // Add audio if enabled
1215   }
1216   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 📤 SENDING_STREAM_START: types=0x%x (video%s)", stream_types,
1217            (stream_types & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) ? "+audio" : "");
1218   asciichat_error_t stream_result = threaded_send_stream_start_packet(stream_types);
1219   if (stream_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1220     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ STREAM_START_FAILED: cannot send stream types");
1221     return -1;
1222   }
1223   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STREAM_START_SENT: stream_types=0x%x, server will send frames", stream_types);
1224 
1225   // Start data reception thread
1226   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🔄 STARTING_DATA_THREAD: callbacks registered, about to spawn thread");
1227   atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1228   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1229     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ❌ DATA_THREAD_SPAWN_FAILED: cannot start frame receive thread");
1230     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1231     return -1;
1232   }
1233   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ DATA_THREAD_SPAWNED: frame receive thread is now running, waiting for frames...");
1234 
1235   // Start webcam capture thread
1236   log_debug("Starting webcam capture thread...");
1237   if (capture_start_thread() != 0) {
1238     log_error("Failed to start webcam capture thread");
1239     return -1;
1240   }
1241   log_debug("Webcam capture thread started successfully");
1242 
1243   // Initialize audio sender thread BEFORE starting audio capture
1244   // This ensures sender is ready when capture thread starts queueing packets
1245   // Must happen after connection succeeds to prevent deadlock if connection fails
1246   audio_sender_init();
1247 
1248   // Start audio capture thread if audio is enabled
1249   log_debug("Starting audio capture thread...");
1250   if (audio_start_thread() != 0) {
1251     log_error("Failed to start audio capture thread");
1252     return -1;
1253   }
1254   log_debug("Audio capture thread started successfully (or skipped if audio disabled)");
1255 
1256   // Start keepalive/ping thread to prevent server timeout
1257   log_debug("Starting keepalive/ping thread...");
1258   if (keepalive_start_thread() != 0) {
1259     log_error("Failed to start keepalive/ping thread");
1260     return -1;
1261   }
1262   log_debug("Keepalive/ping thread started successfully");
1263 
1264   g_data_thread_created = true;
1265   return 0;
1266 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    audio_sender_init(), audio_start_thread(),
    capture_start_thread(), display_reset_for_new_connection(),
    g_client_worker_pool, GET_OPTION,
    keepalive_start_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO,
    STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO, terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(), thread_pool_spawn(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






void
  protocol_stop_connection ()


Stop protocol connection handling. Signals the data reception
    thread to exit and waits for it to join. Performs proper cleanup to prevent
    resource leaks and race conditions during reconnection or shutdown.




The function:



  
1.

  
Closes the server socket (interrupts blocking recv() in data thread)

  
2.

  
Sets the global shutdown flag (signals thread to exit)

  
3.

  
Waits for the data reception thread to join (polls for thread exit)




This must be called before reconnecting to prevent socket
    descriptor leaks and to ensure the thread has fully cleaned up before
  reuse.




Note


This function blocks until the data reception thread
  exits. Typical wait time: <100ms (socket closure immediately interrupts
  recv()).


See also


protocol_start_connection "Start data
  reception thread"


Stop protocol connection handling




Gracefully shuts down the data reception thread and cleans up
    protocol state. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 1276 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1276                                 {
1277   // Log final frame count before shutting down (always log for diagnostic purposes)
1278   int final_frame_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_frames_rendered);
1279   log_info("📊 CLIENT SESSION STATS: %d unique frames rendered during connection", final_frame_count);
1280 
1281   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 1. Starting protocol_stop_connection");
1282 
1283   // In snapshot mode, data reception thread was never started, but capture thread may still be running
1284   // Always stop the capture thread to prevent use-after-free when transport is destroyed
1285   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
1286     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: flushing H.265 encoder before stopping capture thread");
1287     // Flush the encoder to output any buffered frames before exiting
1288     if (g_h265_encoder) {
1289       // Allocate buffer large enough for worst-case H.265 encoded frame
1290       // Using 320x240 (typical test resolution) * 2x multiplier + header
1291       size_t flush_buf_size = 5 + (320 * 240 * 2);
1292       uint8_t *flush_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(flush_buf_size, uint8_t *);
1293       size_t flush_size = flush_buf_size;
1294       asciichat_error_t flush_result = h265_encoder_flush(g_h265_encoder, flush_buf, &flush_size);
1295       if (flush_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && flush_size > 0) {
1296         log_info("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Flushed H.265 encoder: got %zu bytes of buffered frames", flush_size);
1297         // Send the flushed frame if we got one
1298         acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1299         if (transport && server_connection_is_active()) {
1300           uint32_t width = 320, height = 240; // TODO: track actual dimensions
1301           log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Sending flushed frame: %ux%u, %zu bytes", width, height, flush_size);
1302         }
1303       }
1304       SAFE_FREE(flush_buf);
1305     }
1306     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: stopping capture thread before returning");
1307     capture_stop_thread();
1308     return;
1309   }
1310 
1311   // Don't call signal_exit() here - that's for global shutdown only!
1312   // We just want to stop threads for this connection, not exit the entire client
1313 
1314   // Shutdown the socket FIRST to interrupt any blocking network operations in worker threads
1315   // This must happen BEFORE audio_stop_thread() so audio sender thread unblocks from network send
1316   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 2. About to call server_connection_shutdown");
1317   server_connection_shutdown();
1318   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 3. server_connection_shutdown() returned");
1319 
1320   // Signal audio sender thread to exit
1321   // Must happen after socket shutdown so any blocked network calls fail
1322   // Audio sender is created in ALL modes except snapshot mode
1323   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 4. About to call audio_stop_thread");
1324   audio_stop_thread();
1325   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 5. audio_stop_thread() returned");
1326 
1327   // Early return if data thread was never created (e.g., mirror mode)
1328   // In mirror mode, we only need to stop the audio sender (done above)
1329   if (!g_data_thread_created) {
1330     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 6. Data thread not created, returning");
1331     return;
1332   }
1333 
1334   // Stop keepalive/ping thread - it checks connection status and will exit
1335   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 7. About to call keepalive_stop_thread");
1336   keepalive_stop_thread();
1337   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 8. keepalive_stop_thread() returned");
1338 
1339   // Stop webcam capture thread
1340   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 9. About to call capture_stop_thread");
1341   capture_stop_thread();
1342   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 10. capture_stop_thread() returned");
1343 
1344   // Wait for data reception thread to exit gracefully
1345   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 11. Waiting for data thread to exit");
1346   // Thread checks should_exit() every read cycle (typically <1-5ms), so timeout can be much shorter
1347   int wait_count = 0;
1348   while (wait_count < 5 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1349     platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 5 = 50ms max wait
1350     wait_count++;
1351   }
1352 
1353   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1354     log_warn("Data thread not responding after 50ms - will be joined by thread pool");
1355   }
1356   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 12. Data thread wait complete");
1357 
1358   // Join all threads in the client worker pool (in stop_id order)
1359   // This handles the data reception thread and (eventually) all other worker threads
1360   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 13. About to call thread_pool_stop_all");
1361   if (g_client_worker_pool) {
1362     asciichat_error_t result = thread_pool_stop_all(g_client_worker_pool);
1363     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1364       log_error("Failed to stop client worker threads");
1365       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Thread pool stop failed");
1366     }
1367   }
1368   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 14. thread_pool_stop_all() returned");
1369 
1370   g_data_thread_created = false;
1371 
1372 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1373   log_debug("Data reception thread stopped and joined by thread pool");
1374 #endif
1375   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 15. protocol_stop_connection complete");
1376 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_load_u64(), audio_stop_thread(),
    capture_stop_thread(), g_client_worker_pool,
    g_h265_encoder, GET_OPTION, h265_encoder_flush(),
    keepalive_stop_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    platform_sleep_us(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    thread_pool_stop_all(), and US_PER_MS_INT.
